prompt:
Design a StudyRoomOps Reservation & Member Engagement Portal to manage offline study-room booking, access oversight, and on-site community feedback in a single, staff-operated web experience. Regular Users create a local account with a username and password, browse an availability calendar by room and zone, and place reservations in 15-minute increments within configurable business hours (for example, 7:00 AM–11:00 PM) while seeing immediate conflict feedback and suggested alternatives when a preferred slot is taken. Members can save favorite rooms, share a reservation link internally (copy-to-clipboard for intranet use), and submit ratings plus photo/text reviews and Q&A after check-in; the React interface highlights featured reviews, allows moderators to pin or collapse threads, and shows reputation tiers (New, Trusted, Expert) driven by participation and report history. A collaboration/lead intake screen supports group-study and long-term requests with a structured form (requirements, budget cap like $250.00, availability windows, and a contact phone such as (415) 555-0136), then routes the request through New, In Discussion, Quoted, Confirmed, and Closed with visual status badges, internal notes, and local file attachments. Staff roles include Creator (room/zone setup), Moderator (UGC governance), and Administrator (policies, membership tiers, and reporting); users receive in-app reminders for SLA timers, such as flagging leads with no update after 24 hours and reservations awaiting check-in 10 minutes past start time.
The backend uses Express to expose offline REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend, with MongoDB persisting users, roles, reservations, room calendars, UGC objects, leads, membership ledgers, and audit trails. The conflict-detection engine enforces strong consistency by using atomic updates and optimistic concurrency checks so two kiosks cannot book the same seat; it returns a specific conflict reason and computes alternative slots within ±60 minutes. Face detection, recognition, and tracking runs locally using OpenCV on connected cameras, performing frame-level detection, landmark alignment, feature matching, and multi-target tracking; recognition decisions require a configurable confidence threshold (default 0.82) and apply allowlist/blocklist policies, while storing only encrypted face embeddings and short-lived event logs (for example, retained 30 days) rather than raw video. Content safety includes sensitive-word filtering, anti-spam limits (max 5 posts per hour and 20 per day), a report-and-appeal workflow, and reserved extension points for on-prem image moderation without requiring any network calls. Security includes HTTPS within the local network, JWT session expiry of 30 minutes idle/12 hours absolute, CSRF protection, upload format/size validation (JPEG/PNG/PDF up to 10 MB) with file hashing for tamper detection, field-level encryption for sensitive profile data, and immutable operation audit logs; analytics compute booking conversion, attendance and no-show rates, peak/off-peak utilization, and policy impact comparisons with export to CSV and API access filtered by room, zone, and time range. Membership supports tier benefits, points rules (for example, 1 point per $1.00 spent, redeemable in 100-point blocks), stored-value top-up/spend/refund with daily risk limits (such as $200.00), and blacklist controls for repeated no-shows or charge disputes.
